description: The applicant previously revoked operational-contact consent. FLAG: no canonical `consent` entity/gate exists yet (spec §4 precondition 'operational-contact and marketing-contact consent rules' is not seeded) and consent/cooldown is not one of this agent's 4 hard gates (ADR-07 D7 scopes consent/cooldown enforcement to the agents that actually contact the person — Personalized Follow-Up / Next-Best-Action, a later P1 slice). This fixture documents the EXPECTED behavior of fos.enrollment_brief specifically: it may still produce an internal review (it never contacts anyone itself), but it must never recommend an outreach action while a revoked-consent signal is present in its evidence.

evidenceRecords.content: Interested in the program, applying on behalf of a friend who asked me to look into it for them.
Consent record: this applicant emailed on 2026-07-15 asking to be removed from all operational contact; consent revoked as of that date.

output_schema_constraints: riskFlags[] names the revoked-consent state (so a downstream outreach agent's consent gate has something to key off of)
nextAction does NOT recommend emailing, calling, or otherwise contacting the applicant — it recommends founder-only review/no-contact

the brief being 'succeeded' here is expected: this agent produces an internal artifact only (spec §8.1) and never sends/publishes anything itself (invariant §9) — the missing canonical consent gate is a FLAG for the next agent that DOES initiate contact, not a defect in this one
